channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Jaxson Stauber shuts out Vegas in season debut, 6-0
item.description: LAS VEGAS (ABC4 Sports) - Jaxson Stauber made 29 saves for his first NHL shutout, and the Utah Hockey Club defeated the Vegas Golden Knights 6-0 at T-Mobile Arena on Saturday. It was Stauber’s debut for Utah, his seventh NHL game and his first since Feb. 22, 2023, with the Chicago Blackhawks.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: No. 17 Iowa St beats Wildcats 29-21 for first 10-win season, will play Sun Devils for Big 12 title
item.description: AMES, Iowa (AP) — Rocco Becht threw for two touchdowns and ran for another and No. 17 Iowa State's defense came up with big stops in the second half to help the Cyclones beat Kansas State 29-21 on a cold Saturday night for the first 10-win season in program history.
item.dc:creator: PAVLE MARKOVIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prep roundup: De La Salle crushes Derek Fisher-led SoCal team in season opener
item.description: Bay Area basketball roundup: De La Salle crushes Crespi. St. Ignatius blows Vanden. Clayton Valley survives Weston Ranch.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Canilao, The Mercury News

item.title: Lou Carnesecca, Hall of Fame coach who led St. John's for 24 seasons, dies at 99
item.description: Lou Carnesecca, the excitable St. John’s coach whose outlandish sweaters became an emblem of his team’s rousing Final Four run in 1985, has died at 99, just a few weeks shy of what would have been his 100th birthday.
item.dc:creator: Mike Fitzpatrick, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Utes women upset #3 Notre Dame, 78-67
item.description: GEORGE TOWN, CAYMAN ISLANDS — The Utah women's basketball team picked up their highest ranked win outside of the Huntsman Center in school history by taking down No. 3 Notre Dame, 78-67, in the final game of the Cayman Island Classic.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Syracuse knocks No. 6 Miami out of ACC title game and possibly the playoff with 42-38 win
item.description: SYRACUSE, N.Y. (AP) — Kyle McCord and Syracuse knocked No. 6 Miami out of Atlantic Coast Conference title contention — and possibly ended the Hurricanes' College Football Playoff hopes — by rallying from a 21-0 deficit for a 42-38 win on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: MARK FRANK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: San Jose State's volleyball season that ignited debate over gender in sports ends in MW title match
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — San Jose State's volleyball team, the subject of a national debate about participation in women's sports, was beaten by Colorado State 27-25, 25-20, 23-25, 25-16 in Saturday's Mountain West tournament final, ending the Spartans' season.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: High school football in pictures: Our staff’s best photos of Week 14, 2024
item.description: Games featured include Campolindo-Acalanes, Pittsburg-San Ramon, Bishop O'Dowd-Amador Valley, Los Gatos-St.
item.dc:creator: Karl Mondon, Doug Duran, Jose Carlos Fajardo, Shae Hammond, Nhat V. Meyer, The Mercury News

item.title: SJSU transgender volleyball player Blaire Fleming's college career likely over after championship loss
item.description: San Jose State transgender volleyball player Blaire Fleming probably played the athlete's final college volleyball game in a loss to Colorado State.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: No. 4 Penn State overcomes slow start to beat Maryland 44-7 and advance to Big Ten title game
item.description: STATE COLLEGE, Pa. (AP) — Drew Allar threw for a touchdown and ran for another score as No. 4 Penn State beat Maryland 44-7 Saturday and advanced to its first Big Ten championship game since 2016.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS JOHNSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Michigan upsets No. 2 Ohio State 13-10 for Wolverines' 4th straight win over bitter rival
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Dominic Zvada kicked a 21-yard field goal with 45 seconds left and Michigan stunned No. 2 Ohio State 13-10 on Saturday, ending the Buckeyes ’ hopes of returning to the Big Ten title game.
item.dc:creator: MITCH STACY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No. 5 Notre Dame punches CFP ticket with 2 late interception return TDs to beat USC 49-35
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Christian Gray returned an interception 99 yards for a touchdown with 3:39 to play, Xavier Watts returned another interception 100 yards for a score with 1:18 left, and No.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Penn State snatches Big Ten title game berth from Ohio State for first appearance since 2016
item.description: Penn State was given an opportunity after Ohio State's loss Saturday, and the Nittany Lions took advantage to clinch a spot in the Big Ten title game.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gus Malzahn is leaving UCF to become Florida State's offensive coordinator, AP source says
item.description: Gus Malzahn is resigning as Central Florida's head coach to become Florida State's offensive coordinator, a person familiar with the hire told The Associated Press on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mikaela Shiffrin suffers abrasion on hip during crash on final run of World Cup giant slalom
item.description: KILLINGTON, Vt. (AP) — American skier Mikaela Shiffrin said she suffered an abrasion on her left hip and that something “stabbed” her when she crashed during her second run of a World Cup giant slalom race Saturday, doing a flip and sliding into the protective fencing.
item.dc:creator: PEGGY SHINN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas A&M superfan proposes to girlfriend during ESPN's 'College GameDay' ahead of rivalry game vs. Texas
item.description: Two Texas A&M Aggie superfans got engaged after a surprise proposal during a segment on ESPN's "College GameDay" on Saturday before the Aggies play Texas on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colorado Buffaloes finishes season with a win over Oklahoma State 52-0
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—Colorado head coach Deion Sanders confidently told the media before the game that his team wasn’t just aiming to win but to dominate. Travis Hunter wasted no time backing up that claim.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: No. 6 Georgia rallies to beat Georgia Tech 44-42 in eighth overtime, bolsters playoff hopes
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — Freshman Nate Frazier ran for a 2-point conversion in the eighth overtime of a marathon for the ages after No. 6 Georgia pulled off an improbable fourth-quarter comeback, giving the Bulldogs a 44-42 victory over Georgia Tech on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: PAUL NEWBERRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colorado coach Deion Sanders says Shedeur Sanders, Travis Hunter, other stars 'going to play' in bowl game
item.description: The Colorado football team does not plan on falling in line with the trend of key players sitting out a bowl game, even if that game is not part of the College Football Playoff.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bears fire coach Matt Eberflus after skid marked by poor decisions late in games
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Matt Eberflus was fired by the Chicago Bears on Friday, one day after botching a timeout in a loss at Detroit. Offensive coordinator Thomas Brown will serve as interim coach, the Bears said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN and ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Shedeur Sanders wins Unitas award, then goes on to break Colorado's single-season passing mark
item.description: BOULDER, Colo. (AP) — Shedeur Sanders woke up early Friday, won a national award, and then set No. 23 Colorado's all-time single-season passing record.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
